title: Watch status
description: P8's per-feed health substrate: written by pipeline/watcher/run.py at the end of every watcher run, one entry per feed, keyed by feed id. This is the missing link between the watcher and the audit -- pipeline/watcher/run.py's per-feed failures previously evaporated into stdout, and pipeline/audit/feed_health.py's check_feed_coverage could not mechanically distinguish 'quiet' (reachable, parsing fine, nothing new) from 'broken' (fetch failing, or a page/sitemap/API structurally changed out from under its configured selector). Fields are transition-keyed only ('status' + 'status_since', no per-run counters) so a stretch of identical daily outcomes writes byte-identical JSON -- see pipeline/watcher/jsonio.py's write_if_changed contract ('re-run adds nothing'). Lives at data/{jurisdiction_id}/watch_status.json since the registry-model pivot; top-level 'jurisdiction_id' must match the file's own directory and config/site.json's jurisdictions[].id.

mechanism.description: The dispatch mechanism this feed used this run (pipeline/watcher/mechanisms.DISPATCH key), e.g. 'rss', 'atom', 'html_diff', 'sitemap_diff', 'json_api' -- or an unrecognized value from a config typo, which is exactly what status=='config_error' reports.

status_since.description: Timestamp of the run where this feed's status last transitioned; preserved unchanged across consecutive runs with the same status, so streak length is derivable as a date difference without storing a counter.

last_error.description: The mechanism's own error message on the most recent run, or null when status=='ok'.
